solution:

class CombinationIterator:
    def __init__(self, characters: str, combinationLength: int) -> None:
        self.characters = characters
        self.combinationLength = combinationLength
        self.index = 0
        self.length = len(self.characters)

    def next(self) -> str:
        if self.index >= self.length - self.combinationLength:
            return None
        result = ""
        while self.index < self.length:
            result += self.characters[self.index]
            self.index += 1
        return result

    def hasNext(self) -> bool:
        return self.index < self.length - self.combinationLength

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The solution uses a while loop to iterate until the index reaches the desired combination length. It checks if there are more combinations to return None if there are no more combinations. The time complexity is O(n) because the loop runs n times, where n is the length of the string. The space complexity is O(1) because the algorithm uses a constant amount of space for the result string and index variable.
